response.justification: Candidate is a strong polyglot senior engineer with extensive web, backend, and tooling experience, but the resume shows minimal recent Java work and no demonstrated depth in Kotlin, Spring, JVM internals, or IDE/language tooling — the core requirements of this role. Java appears only briefly (robotic arms, mobile orchestration) years ago, and the closest IDE-adjacent work (LabWindows/CVI in C/C++, 2014-2015) is dated and not JVM-related. Fit for the specific enterprise JVM tooling focus is weak despite overall seniority.
strengths: Senior-level experience on complex, large-scale systems across multiple companies
Some prior IDE/tooling exposure (LabWindows/CVI at National Instruments) and developer-tooling work at Naver
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, workshops and teaching
concerns: No demonstrated depth in Kotlin, Spring, Hibernate, or Quarkus
Java experience is limited and dated, with no recent JVM-heavy roles
No evidence of IDE internals or language tooling work on the JVM

key_factors.factor: Senior engineering breadth and tooling interest
